title: Americans to begin saying their goodbyes to George H.W. Bush
summary: Americans will begin saying goodbye to former President George H.W. Bush on Monday when his body arrives in Washington for public viewing in the Capitol Rotunda.

title: Civil emergency declaration extended as roads, water lines take priority after quake
summary: Two days after the earthquake, repairing damaged roads and fixing water line ruptures remain the biggest infrastructure priorities.

title: ‘We’re all shaken up': It’s normal to feel scared, anxious and helpless after a big quake. Here’s what to do about it.
summary: It’s not just you. People all over Southcentral Alaska had trouble sleeping, leaped at every aftershock and woke today with a kind of earthquake hangover.

title: Alaska Railroad reports impassable track north of Anchorage after earthquake
summary: A railroad spokesman said he did not know when train service would resume between Anchorage and Fairbanks.

title: Strict building codes helped Anchorage withstand quake
summary: The magnitude 7.0 earthquake that rattled Alaska’s largest city cracked roads and collapsed highway ramps, but there were no reports of widespread catastrophic damage or collapsed buildings.

title: 'Returning to normal’: Earthquake damage assessments continue amid aftershocks
summary: While the 7.0 quake caused no deaths, major injuries or catastrophic building collapses, it wreaked havoc on transportation networks north of Anchorage.

title: Anchorage fire chief: How to look for serious structural damage at home
summary: The city says it hopes to have more information soon on how homeowners can get help assessing structural damage.

title: Anchorage fire chief: How to look for serious structural damage at home
summary: The city says it hopes to have more information soon on how homeowners can get help assessing structural damage.

title: Aftershocks continue across Southcentral Alaska after 7.0 earthquake
summary: They’re expected to continue for some time, though are generally expected to diminish in intensity.

title: At 8:29 Friday morning, everyone emerged from Alaska’s big earthquake with a story to tell
summary: People across Southcentral Alaska ducked under desks, ran down stairwells, pulled cars off the road and huddled with co-workers in parking lots after the quake hit.

title: At 8:29 Friday morning, everyone emerged from Alaska’s big earthquake with a story to tell
summary: People across Southcentral Alaska ducked under desks, ran down stairwells, pulled cars off the road and huddled with co-workers in parking lots after the quake hit.

title: Earthquake rattles shelves and shatters products at Anchorage businesses
summary: Despite the mess around town, one store owner said “there was never a hesitation to stay open.”

title: Quake was Anchorage’s most significant since 1964
summary: The event triggered dozens of aftershocks, including many greater than magnitude 4.5.

title: Utilities will work through night to fix widespread gas leaks and outages after 7.0 earthquake
summary: Some 30,000 customers lost power in the Anchorage area, and the natural gas company was responding to more than 300 reports of leaks.

title: President Trump tweets encouragement to Alaska after earthquake
summary: The president is in Argentina for the G20 summit.

title: Injuries, road damage and gas leaks reported in Mat-Su after 7.0 earthquake
summary: The only hospital in the borough was seeing ‘scattered’ injuries on Friday.

title: Glenn Highway reopens to traffic in both directions as delays continue
summary: Police had closed the highway to southbound traffic for four hours to allow rush-hour traffic north out of Anchorage.

title: Inspections underway across Southcentral Alaska for structural damage after earthquake, aftershocks
summary: Authorities were deploying planes, drones and engineers on foot to get a handle on the scale of structural damage caused by the morning’s 7.0 magnitude earthquake.
